title: helianthemum origanifolium subsp. glabratum
intro: Helianthemum origanifolium subsp. glabratum is a subspecies of the rockrose plant, native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, star-shaped flowers in shades of pink, purple, or white, and its fragrant, oval-shaped leaves with a distinctive sweet, anise-like aroma. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Helianthemum origanifolium subsp. glabratum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
